response.justification: Candidate brings a Staff-level title with direct AI-agent and MCP tooling work, plus prior high-throughput distributed-systems experience (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP req/s) and strong TypeScript/Node.js craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, Python — a must-have — is absent from both the skills list and project history, and the resume leans heavily on DLang and JS/TS. Strategy-level scope and stakeholder breadth are implied but not explicitly demonstrated. Worth a screening call to confirm Python depth and scale of strategic ownership before progressing.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code-generator work at current Staff role
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, public workshops/talks
concerns: No evidence of Python anywhere in skills, employment, or projects (must-have)
Strategy/stakeholder-management scope at Staff level is asserted by title but not detailed
Heavy DLang specialization and many short tenures may signal narrower modern-stack depth than required
